description: Runtime context: $input at a cross-lineage merge. $input is the root input event of the current event's lineage; for an event descending from a merge of contributors with different roots, $input is unbound (undefined). Here two caller writes (two lineages) merge in a no-condition buffer; a downstream transform reads $input, which is undefined because the contributors disagree on a root, so the expression's result is undefined and the node fails with TRANSFORM_UNDEFINED per the Transforms rule. The single-write common case where $input is a defined constant is already exercised by OG-EX-01 ($input.filter).

fixtures.name: $input is undefined for an event merged from disagreeing lineages
fixtures.description: Two caller writes each root a lineage. A no-condition buffer accumulates both across lineages and flushes one array event whose contributors have different roots, so $input is unbound for it. The downstream transform's expression is bare $input, which evaluates to undefined, failing the node with TRANSFORM_UNDEFINED; onError routes the error event (carrying the merged array as its event) to output. This pins the merge clause of Runtime context: $input is defined iff all contributors share one root.
